rationale_top3: BIG4 Caloundra Waterfront has earned Gold status through an exceptional combination of resort-style facilities and spotless presentation that families rave about. The waterslides and heated pool complex are described as "next level" and "resort quality," with countless reviews noting kids didn't want to leave. The camp kitchen is repeatedly called "the best ever seen" and "like a five-star restaurant," while cleanliness standards across amenities are described as "immaculate," "spotless," and "sparkling clean no matter the time of day."
rationale_honourable: The park's waterfront location on Pumicestone Passage delivers direct access to coastal walkways, Bulcock Beach, and Caloundra's shopping and dining precinct across the road. The onsite Tripcony Traders restaurant and Tom & Andy's coffee van provide quality food service, with poolside cocktail delivery creating a true holiday resort atmosphere. Families particularly praise the friendly, professional staff, organized kids activities, and the combination of jumping pillow, pedal karts, and go-karts that keep children entertained for hours beyond the spectacular pool area.
water_fun: Resort-quality heated pool with two waterslides
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal karts, daily activities
pet_detail: Dog-friendly with $10 per night fee; no dedicated dog facilities mentioned
best_for: This park is best for families with young children who want a luxury caravan park experience with exceptional pool facilities, immaculate cleanliness, and the convenience of walking to beaches, shops, and restaurants without needing to drive.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Caloundra Waterfront Holiday Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-26
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 440

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently highlight the "resort-quality" heated pool with two waterslides that kids and adults love, jumping pillow, pedal karts, go-karts, and organized daily activities including discos, bingo, and kids entertainment coordinated by staff member Nick who is "amazing with kids." Families report children "didn't want to leave" and spent hours entertained.

Nature (17/20) — The park sits on Pumicestone Passage waterfront with "stunning views" and direct gate access to coastal walking/cycling paths, Bulcock Beach "just around the corner," and beautiful morning beach walks. Gardens are "beautifully maintained" with local wildlife, though it's more waterfront urban than bushland setting.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelmingly praised as "the cleanest campground I have stayed at" with amenities "always spotless," "immaculate," and "sparkling clean no matter the time of day." Camp kitchen described as "like a five-star restaurant" and villas "exceptionally clean." Staff dedication to maintenance is evident throughout reviews.

Value (11/15) — Premium pricing at $100+ per powered site and $350+ per night for cabins is noted as "expensive," "pricey," and "out of touch with caravan affordability" by some guests. However, many feel "you get what you pay for" given the resort-style facilities, location, and quality. Some feel pricing doesn't match value compared to other parks.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites described as "adequate," "reasonable size," and "spacious" on premium sites with artificial grass. Some areas have "tight" sites where "caravans hang over onto the road" and "neighbours congregate on the road because sites aren't big enough." Drive-through sites and greenery between sites are appreciated.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional repeat visitation with families returning annually for 3-12 years. Reviews express "absolute 10/10," "can't wait to come back," and "already re-booked for next year." Staff praised as "friendly," "professional," and "going above and beyond." Strong recommendation rate from families.

Location (9/10) — Perfect urban convenience with Woolworths/Coles "across the road," walking distance to Caloundra CBD, beaches, restaurants, and cafes. "Everything at your fingertips" and "literally outside the gate." Central Sunshine Coast location for attractions like Australia Zoo. Some road noise from busy intersection affects sites near boundary.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Premium pricing ($100+ powered sites, $350+ cabins) is considered expensive and "out of reach" by some long-term guests, with one reviewer noting an $800 increase year-over-year that feels "out of touch with caravan affordability."
2. Site sizes vary significantly across the park, with some areas having tight, cramped sites where vans overhang roads and cars must park in overflow areas, while only premium sites offer generous space.
3. Traffic noise from the busy Bulcock/Bowman intersection affects sites along the back boundary, with constant road noise until midnight requiring air conditioning to block it out, and single-key access to amenities creates inconvenience for families.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Simply the Best Caravan park in Queensland", "camp kitchen is like a five star restaurant", "cleanest campground I have stayed at", "feels like you're staying at an all inclusive holiday resort", "kids absolutely loved the water park and didn't want to leave", "resort quality pool and restaurant", "staff are so lovely and helpful", "one of the best parks we've ever stayed in"

rationale_top3: Exceptional animal experiences dominate positive reviews with daily guinea pig cuddles, deer feeding, and interactive encounters creating magical memories for young children. Ranger Steve and his family receive extraordinary praise for their warmth, knowledge, and dedication to educational experiences that drive exceptional repeat visitation. Stunning Sunshine Coast Hinterland setting with valley views, access to Mary River and Booloumba Creek swimming spots, Jurassic Trail walks, and abundant native wildlife. The peaceful natural environment combined with spacious, well-spaced sites allows families to genuinely disconnect while children safely explore. Exceptionally clean amenities with hot showers and spotless facilities, combined with diverse activities including movie nights, kids disco, fairy gardens, and organized events. The wholesome, family-oriented atmosphere with enforced quiet hours creates a safe environment where parents can relax and children can roam freely.
rationale_honourable: Strong value proposition for camping guests with free animal feeding included, generous site sizes accommodating large rigs, and proximity to Kenilworth attractions. Multiple camping areas offer variety and privacy, while the emotional connection created by staff drives remarkable loyalty with families returning annually.
water_fun: Creek and river swimming nearby, no pool
kids_play: Animal feeding, guinea pigs, fairy garden, trails
pet_detail: Dog friendly - dogs welcome at campsites
best_for: Young families wanting hands-on animal experiences, nature exploration, and a genuinely relaxing farm-stay atmosphere where children can safely roam and parents can disconnect from daily stress.
executive_summary:
PARK: Kookaburra Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 142

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews consistently highlight exceptional animal experiences as the star attraction: daily guinea pig cuddles and feeding, deer interactions, goats, pigs, geese, and chickens. Ranger Steve and his children receive extraordinary praise for making animal encounters educational and magical. Additional activities include movie nights in the barn, kids disco events, the Jurassic Trail bushwalk, fairy garden with cubby house, basketball, volleyball, and organized Saturday night activities including food trucks. Families report children never running out of things to do across multi-day stays.

Nature (18/20) — Set in stunning Sunshine Coast Hinterland with valley views and mountain surrounds. Easy access to Mary River for swimming and kayaking, proximity to Booloumba Creek (10 minutes) with crystal clear waters, Little Yabba Creek, and multiple nature walks including the 1.2km Jurassic Trail with panoramic lookout. Abundant native wildlife including wallabies, kookaburras, and diverse birdlife. Reviews describe the setting as peaceful, green, and perfect for disconnecting from busy life while remaining close to Kenilworth town and attractions.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback describes amenities as exceptionally clean with hot showers, well-maintained toilets, and facilities kept spotless throughout stays. The Barn amenities block receives particular praise. One older shower block at Pine Tree Hill was noted as run down with maintenance issues (blocked toilet, leaking pipes) but reviews indicate it's scheduled for demolition and replacement. Park grounds are well-mowed and maintained.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing: some reviews mention it being "on the expensive side" particularly for day visits ($35 for non-campers), while camping guests generally find it reasonable for the facilities and experiences provided. Free animal feeding for campers is valued highly. Coffee van on-site, shop with supplies. Several reviews from repeat visitors suggest perceived value improves with longer stays and full engagement with activities.

Site Size (9/10) — Sites consistently described as spacious, generous, well-spaced, and not feeling crowded even when fully booked. Multiple camping areas (Pine Tree Hill, Flat Hill, The Paddock, Damside, Gum Tree Valley, etc.) offer variety. Campers don't feel "on top of each other." Sites accommodate large rigs with power and water available. Some sites offer private, tucked-away locations with stunning views.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional repeat visitation rate with numerous reviews from families returning annually or multiple times. Phrases like "favourite camping spot," "can't wait to come back," "booking our next trip already," and "will definitely be back" dominate. Strong emotional connection evident with descriptions of "heaven," "magical," and "cures burnout." Very high recommendation rate to friends and family. Staff (especially Ranger Steve) create genuine connections that drive loyalty.

Location (6/10) — While the Kenilworth hinterland location offers natural beauty and proximity to swimming holes, it's relatively remote (10 minutes to Kenilworth town, 1 hour to Noosa). No mobile reception with Vodafone on-site. Location is intentionally secluded which appeals to target market but limits convenience scores. Close to Kenilworth Bakery, Kenilworth Dairies, and natural attractions but requires driving for supplies and activities outside the park.

WHY NOT 100:
1. The park's remote hinterland location means limited mobile reception and genuine isolation from conveniences, requiring 10+ minute drives to Kenilworth for supplies or bakery runs, which may challenge families needing connectivity or easy access to amenities.

2. Value perception issues exist with day-visitor pricing ($35 for animal viewing) receiving harsh criticism as a "scam" from non-camping guests, and some camping families noting costs are "on the expensive side" compared to alternatives, suggesting pricing could be more competitive.

3. Maintenance inconsistencies appeared in 2025-2026 with the Pine Tree Hill amenities block described as run down with blocked toilets, broken showers, and flooding laundry facilities, and some reviews noting overgrown grounds creating tick/snake concerns, though improvements are reportedly underway.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"guinea pig cuddles were the kid's favourite", "Steve and his family are wonderful hosts", "magical, a little piece of heaven on earth", "best camping experience we have had", "this place cures burnout", "cleanest camping grounds we've stayed at", "incredibly family-friendly with plenty of space", "peaceful, wholesome vibe", "kids absolutely loved the animal feeding", "we go back every year now"

rationale_top3: Ingenia Holidays Rivershore delivers an exceptional family holiday experience with resort-style amenities that keep children entertained for days. The heated pool with waterslides is the undisputed star, complemented by go-karts, mini golf, jumping pillow, movie nights, kids discos, archery, animal encounters, and scavenger hunts—all creating a cruise-ship atmosphere without leaving the park. Staff consistently receive praise for being friendly, accommodating, and genuinely invested in guest experiences, with several reviewers naming specific team members who went above and beyond. The riverfront location provides a peaceful natural setting with excellent fishing opportunities, boat ramp access, and spacious grassy sites. Glamping tents are repeatedly described as luxurious with claw foot baths, comfortable beds, and river views. Families appreciate the bar and restaurant overlooking the pool, allowing parents to supervise while relaxing.
rationale_honourable: The park's SMS communication system keeps guests informed of daily activities and specials, adding to the professional, well-organized feel that families value. Sites are repeatedly described as huge and spacious, with even 30ft caravans fitting easily. The central Sunshine Coast location provides easy access to attractions like the Big Pineapple and beaches within 15 minutes.
water_fun: Heated pool with two waterslides and splash pad
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, go-karts, mini golf, activities
pet_detail: Dog friendly on leash, allowed on ground floor of bar/bistro area
best_for: This park is best for families with primary school-aged children who want a full entertainment program, water-based fun, and the option to barely leave the park during their stay.
executive_summary:
PARK: Ingenia Holidays Rivershore
SCORE: 87/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-22
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 296

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Consistently praised for exceptional kids entertainment including heated pool with waterslides, jumping pillow, go-karts, mini golf, pedal carts, archery, animal encounters, kids discos, movie nights, and scavenger hunts. Families describe it as "cruise-ship" atmosphere with daily activity planners during school holidays. Pool is the standout feature mentioned in majority of reviews.

Nature (16/20) — Beautiful riverfront location with pontoon access for fishing and crabbing, grassy sites with river views, boat ramp, and peaceful natural setting. However, proximity to main road diminishes the natural tranquility. Described as "paradise" by multiple families with stunning sunrise/sunset views over the water.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive comments about clean, modern amenities with multiple toilet/shower blocks kept "spotless" and "immaculate." Some reviews noted inconsistent bathroom cleaning frequency (once daily rather than continuous), occasional maintenance lapses, and declining standards over time according to repeat visitors.

Value (12/15) — Mixed feedback on pricing. Powered sites $87-$200+ per night and glamping tents $400-$580 per night described as "expensive" and "over the top" by several families. However, many felt the extensive included activities, resort facilities, and staff service justified premium pricing. Restaurant food reasonably priced with happy hour specials appreciated.

Site Size (9/10) — Repeatedly described as "huge," "massive," and "extremely spacious" sites that could fit multiple caravans. Even 30ft caravans fit easily. Unpowered riverfront sites particularly praised for size. Some deluxe sites near playground noted as noisier locations.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high satisfaction with phrases like "absolute dream come true," "heaven on earth," "best park ever," and "couldn't fault a single thing" appearing frequently. Strong repeat visitation with families booking return stays immediately. However, scattered complaints about rude reception staff (specifically named), poor pool slide supervision, and declining standards reduced perfect sentiment score.

Location (8/10) — Centrally located 15 minutes from Big Pineapple, Sea Life aquarium, and beaches. Close to Bli Bli shops (4km) and Maroochydore (7km). Easy highway access for day trips. However, direct road noise is a significant negative mentioned by multiple reviewers. Not walkable to attractions but convenient base camp for exploring Sunshine Coast hinterland.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Road noise from David Low Way is a recurring complaint, with several families reporting traffic noise disrupting sleep and describing it as "extreme" and "destroyed all the beauty" of the riverside location—some even cancelled their stay early due to this issue.

2. Value concerns are significant, with families paying $200-$250 per night for powered sites and $400-$580 for glamping tents questioning whether the experience justifies these premium prices, particularly when compared to other Queensland parks. Weekend surcharges of 15% on food also mentioned negatively.

3. Service inconsistency appears in multiple reviews, with specific staff members described as "extremely rude," "abrupt," and "unwilling to help" at reception. Pool slide supervision noted as inadequate with safety concerns, and bathroom cleaning frequency dropped from continuous to once daily, suggesting operational standards have slipped.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"heated pool and waterslides were a huge hit," "staff went above and beyond," "safari tents were incredible," "absolute heaven on earth for the whole family," "resort style feeling," "one of the best parks we've stayed at," "traffic noise all night destroyed all the beauty," "extremely rude reception staff," "expensive but worth it for the facilities," "kids never stopped smiling," "spotless facilities," "riverfront access ideal for fishing"

rationale_top3:
Landsborough Holiday Park (Ingenia Rivershore) delivers exceptional family entertainment with its heated resort-style pool featuring two waterslides that families consistently describe as 'ridiculously fun' and a major highlight. The park offers daily activities including archery, animal encounters, kids discos, movie nights, mini golf, pedal karts, and a jumping pillow—all creating what families call 'one of the best holiday parks ever' with children never wanting to leave.

The natural setting alongside the river provides fishing access, a pontoon for crabbing, and peaceful riverside glamping sites with stunning views. Families love the combination of resort amenities with nature, describing it as having 'all the camping feels with glamping luxuries.' The park's location in the Sunshine Coast Hinterland offers quick access to Australia Zoo and beaches while maintaining a tranquil setting.

Facilities are consistently praised as 'spotless,' 'immaculate,' and 'always clean and well maintained' with exceptionally spacious sites described as 'the biggest I've ever stayed at.' Staff receive overwhelming praise for being 'friendly,' 'helpful,' and going 'above and beyond,' contributing to families describing this as their 'regular family camping spot' worth returning to repeatedly.
rationale_honourable: The park's spacious sites accommodate large caravans with room to spare, and the combination of free daily activities with resort-style amenities creates exceptional value despite premium pricing. The riverside setting provides peaceful nature immersion while remaining conveniently located just 15 minutes from beaches and 5 minutes from Australia Zoo.
water_fun: Heated pool with two slides, toddler splash area
kids_play: Playground, jumping pillow, mini golf, pedal karts available
pet_detail: Dog friendly park with dogs permitted on ground floor of bar/bistro area on leash
best_for: Active families with young children who want resort-style amenities, waterpark fun, and organized daily activities combined with riverside camping and nature access.
executive_summary:
PARK: Landsborough Holiday Park (Ingenia Rivershore)
SCORE: 84/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 500

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (18/20) — Reviews consistently praise the heated resort-style pool with two waterslides as 'ridiculously fun' and a major highlight. Daily activities include archery, animal encounters, kids discos, movie nights, scavenger hunts, mini golf, pedal karts, and jumping pillow—all included at no extra cost. Families describe children spending 'all day having fun' with activities keeping them entertained throughout their stay. Some reviews note kids activities during school holidays could have more staff enthusiasm.

Nature (17/20) — The park sits alongside a beautiful river with fishing access, a pontoon for crabbing, and peaceful riverside views. Families love the natural setting with 'stunning location right next to the river' and opportunities for kayaking and bird watching. Safari glamping tents overlook the river creating a nature-immersed experience. Minor deduction for being in hinterland rather than beachfront, though beaches are 15 minutes away.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Facilities described as 'spotless,' 'immaculate,' and 'always clean and well maintained' across hundreds of reviews. Amenities blocks receive consistent praise for cleanliness with showers 'delightfully hot and free.' Some reviews from school holiday periods note pool-area bathrooms for day visitors needed more frequent cleaning, and one review mentioned female toilets without hand soap for 3 days.

Value (11/15) — Pricing is premium at $200+ per night for powered sites and $580 for glamping tents during peak periods. Many families note 'expensive' or 'overpriced' but consistently add that facilities and activities justify the cost. Food and drinks at restaurant are 'reasonably priced' and happy hour appreciated. Some families feel glamping tents offer excellent value while others find tent pricing too high for what's provided.

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites consistently described as 'huge,' 'very generous,' 'spacious' and 'biggest site I've ever stayed at' with room for large caravans and extra vehicles. One review noted a 9m caravan site 'could have fit another caravan and car on it.' Unpowered riverside sites are 'HUGE' according to multiple families. Minor point loss for some deluxe sites near playground being noisier than expected.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelming majority of families express desire to return with phrases like 'will definitely be back,' 'our regular family camping spot,' 'already booked for next year,' and 'couldn't wait to come back.' Staff receive exceptional praise for being 'friendly,' 'helpful,' and going 'above and beyond.' Some negative sentiment around staff rudeness (particularly one reception staff member named Helen), and permanent residents in some reviews expressing frustration with families.

Location (6/10) — Park is centrally located 15 minutes to beaches, 5 minutes to Australia Zoo, and accessible to Sunshine Coast attractions. Families appreciate the convenient location as a 'base camp' for exploring. Major drawback is significant road noise from main highway that 'woke us up on several occasions' and is 'pretty bad' according to multiple reviews. Park is not beachfront and requires driving to coast.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Road noise from the main highway significantly impacts the experience with multiple families reporting being woken at night by traffic and describing it as 'really bad' and an ongoing issue throughout their stay.
2. Premium pricing at $200+ per night for powered sites and up to $580 for glamping makes it one of Queensland's most expensive parks, with value concerns expressed even by families who enjoyed their stay.
3. Staff service inconsistency with several detailed complaints about rude reception staff (particularly during school holidays), lack of enthusiasm in kids activity delivery, and poor handling of guest concerns, contrasting with many positive staff reviews.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"ridiculously fun slides," "one of the best holiday parks ever," "kids never stopped smiling," "staff went above and beyond," "biggest site I've ever stayed at," "spotless and clean," "will definitely be back," "road noise pretty bad," "overpriced but worth it," "perfect for families"

park_name: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
rationale_top3: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park earns Gold status with an outstanding location just minutes from the beach, complemented by exceptionally clean facilities and grounds that families consistently praise. The park's spotless amenities, heated pool, and well-maintained playground create a family-friendly environment, while the proximity to Alexandra Headlands Beach, cafes, shops, and surf club means everything is within easy walking distance. Families particularly appreciate the helpful and friendly staff who go above and beyond, the spacious caravan sites with privacy hedges, and the on-site coffee van and regular food truck visits.
rationale_honourable: The quiet, peaceful atmosphere combined with excellent access to coastal walks, parklands, and the adjoining lake make this park ideal for families seeking both relaxation and beach activities without the noise of main roads. Exceptional repeat visitation rates with families returning annually for 5-15+ years demonstrate strong loyalty and satisfaction.
water_fun: Heated pool with children's wading area
pet_detail: Dog friendly on caravan sites (must be on lead); dogs not permitted on cabin decks
best_for: Best suited for young families who value cleanliness, proximity to the beach, and a quiet park environment with friendly staff and good facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-27
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 253

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews highlight a heated pool with children's wading area, playground, and games room. Families love the on-site coffee van and regular food truck visits (especially wood-fired pizza). Limited structured kids entertainment or activities beyond pool and playground, though proximity to beach and parklands provides natural play opportunities.

Nature (18/20) — Exceptional location with 3-5 minute walk to beautiful Alexandra Headlands Beach, adjoining lake with duck pond and bird life, coastal walkway, and expansive parklands. Families appreciate dog-friendly beach and lake walks, tropical palm tree surroundings, and peaceful setting away from main road noise.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly praised as "spotlessly clean," "immaculate," and "meticulously clean." Amenities consistently described as modern, well-maintained, and cleaned daily. Grounds beautifully kept with manicured gardens. A few isolated complaints about cabin maintenance issues (faulty appliances, holes in screens) but these appear to be exceptions.

Value (14/15) — Described as "great value," "excellent value for money," and "reasonable prices." Special deals like "stay 7 nights, pay for 6" appreciated. Generally seen as affordable for the location and quality, though a few guests felt prices were high for short stays or basic cabins.

Site Size (9/10) — Caravan sites consistently praised as "excellent sized," "spacious," "roomy" with "plenty of room to move around." Privacy hedges between sites highly valued. Double concrete slabs noted. Some cabin guests found rooms compact or cramped, especially for larger families.

Sentiment (9/10) — Extremely high repeat visitation with many families returning annually for 5-15+ years. Strong recommendation rate with phrases like "will definitely be back," "highly recommend," and "best park on the Sunshine Coast." Staff friendliness and helpfulness consistently praised. Few negative experiences related to booking errors or service issues.

Location (10/10) — Perfect scores for location with universal praise for proximity to beach (3-5 min walk), shops, cafes, restaurants, surf club, and shopping centres. Central position between Maroochydore and Mooloolaba ideal for exploring Sunshine Coast. Quiet despite central location.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited organized kids entertainment or structured activities beyond pool and playground—no jumping pillow, waterpark features, or kids club mentioned.
2. Several reports of maintenance issues in older cabins including faulty appliances, poor wifi connectivity, dated fixtures, and small cramped bathrooms, though staff generally responsive to issues.
3. Some operational complaints including inflexible cancellation policies, booking system errors, noise from unsupervised children, and a few reports of poor customer service interactions.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"walking distance to patrolled beach," "spotlessly clean amenities," "heated pool," "staff were very friendly and helpful," "immaculately presented," "great location close to everything," "perfect for families," "coffee van on-site," "quiet and peaceful," "excellent sized caravan sites," "will definitely be back," "highly recommend"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with private gate access to patrolled beaches and walking distance to Mooloolaba esplanade, cafes, shops and attractions - consistently rated as the park's standout feature Immaculately maintained facilities with spotlessly clean amenities, excellent water pressure, and well-kept grounds that receive overwhelmingly positive feedback from families Strong natural setting with outstanding beach access, coastal walks, adjacent parkland with duck pond, and dog-friendly beach nearby for active families
rationale_honourable: Mooloolaba Beach Holiday Park earns Silver for its exceptional location just steps from the beach and Mooloolaba esplanade, with clean modern amenities and a heated pool that families consistently praise. While it lacks extensive on-site entertainment and space compared to larger parks, its walkability to beaches, cafes, restaurants and attractions makes it ideal for families who want an active coastal holiday base rather than a self-contained resort experience.
water_fun: Heated pool for relaxing, no waterpark
kids_play: Small playground, beach is main attraction
pet_detail: Dog friendly on leash, beach access on leash nearby
best_for: Active families who prioritize beach proximity and town access over extensive on-site entertainment, and those who value cleanliness and walkability to coastal attractions.
executive_summary:
PARK: Mooloolaba Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 78/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 500

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews consistently note minimal on-site entertainment with a small playground and games room, but families appreciate the heated pool and mention the beach is the main attraction. Food trucks visit regularly (pizza, Greek, Mexican) which adds convenience. No waterpark or extensive kids club facilities.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding beach access with private gates leading directly to patrolled swimming beaches, beautiful coastal walks, and adjacent parkland with duck pond. Families praised the "3-5 minute walk to beach," "dog-friendly beach nearby," and "lovely parkland" next door for walks and cycling.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Overwhelmingly positive feedback on cleanliness with phrases like "spotlessly clean," "immaculately clean amenities," "excellent water pressure," and "well maintained." A few reviews mentioned amenities not cleaned on some days and facilities being "fairly poor and dirty" in isolated cases, preventing a perfect score.

Value (10/15) — Mixed reviews on value with many calling it "expensive," "very expensive for what you get," "too expensive for pensioners," and noting council greed. Powered sites $68-95/night considered steep for a basic park. However, location proximity saves on transport costs and some found it "good value" given the beach access.

Site Size (8/10) — Sites described as "spacious," "excellent sized," "good concrete slabs," with privacy hedges between most sites. However, multiple reviews noted "small sites," "extremely tight and squishy," "compact," and challenges for larger rigs. En-suite facilities particularly criticized as "extremely small."

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high repeat visitation with many families returning annually for 10-15+ years. Phrases like "love this place," "will definitely be back," "highly recommend," and "can't wait to return" dominate. Friendly staff consistently praised. Some frustration with customer service inflexibility and pricing dampened perfect sentiment.

Location (10/10) — Perfect score for location with universal praise: "walking distance to everything," "close to beach, cafes, shops," "central to everything on Sunshine Coast," "can't beat the location," and "ideal location." Proximity to Mooloolaba esplanade, surf clubs, and patrolled beaches is the park's standout feature.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Limited on-site entertainment and facilities for children beyond a small playground and basic pool - families noted "nothing kid friendly" and minimal activities compared to larger resort parks.
2. Pricing considered high relative to basic amenities offered, with numerous reviews citing expensive rates ($80-95/night) for a council-run park with dated facilities and tight sites.
3. Site size and spacing issues, particularly for larger rigs and families, with multiple mentions of cramped conditions, tight maneuvering, and very small en-suite bathroom facilities that felt inadequate.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:

SCORE: 77/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 416

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews consistently mention jumping pillow, putt putt mini golf, small pool, playground, and excellent school holiday programs with daily activities including discos, reptile shows, science shows, movie nights, and marshmallow roasting at the fire pit. Parents appreciated the Wednesday pancake breakfasts and visiting food trucks, though some noted the pool was "very small" and facilities were more suited to younger children than teenagers.

Nature (14/20) — The park sits directly across from the Maroochy River with easy walking access, river walks, and sunset viewing spots. Families enjoyed the well-maintained gardens, herb gardens, tropical plantings, and shaded trees throughout the park. However, the location is dominated by proximity to the Sunshine Coast Motorway rather than being immersed in nature, with several reviews noting it feels more suburban than natural.

Cleanliness (14/15) — This was the park's standout strength with overwhelmingly consistent praise. Reviews repeatedly used phrases like "cleanest park we've ever stayed in," "immaculately maintained," "spotless amenities," "cleaned twice daily," and "exceptional cleanliness." Staff were frequently named for their meticulous work, particularly Lisa, with amenities, camp kitchens, grounds, and cabins all receiving top marks for presentation.

Value (11/15) — Mixed reviews on value with some families finding it reasonably priced for the Sunshine Coast location and service level, while others felt it was "expensive for what it was" or "not worth the money we paid." Several reviews mentioned the $10/night dog fee seemed excessive, and one reviewer called a $350 cabin rate "ridiculous." The free marriage saver service and Wednesday pancakes were seen as value-adds.

Site Size (8/10) — Caravan sites received mixed feedback, with many praising the artificial turf, level sites, and adequate space, while others noted sites were "very close together," "a bit tight," or required the tractor service because of narrow access. Cabins were described as "spacious" and "well appointed." The park itself is noted as "small" and "intimate" rather than sprawling.

Sentiment (9/10) — Very high repeat visitation rate with many families stating they return regularly, have stayed multiple times, and "won't stay anywhere else." Staff warmth and helpfulness were praised extensively. However, a small number of very negative reviews about noise, size, and specific incidents prevented a perfect sentiment score. Overall recommendation rate is strong among family travellers.

Location (7/10) — Excellent proximity to Sunshine Coast beaches, shopping (Sunshine Plaza), IGA within walking distance, Flume Cafe nearby, and easy motorway access for touring. However, this convenience comes at the cost of constant highway noise that was the single most common complaint, with reviews mentioning "very very loud traffic noise," "like staying in a breakdown lane," and traffic starting at 4am. River access is a short walk across a busy road.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Persistent and significant highway/motorway noise from the adjacent Sunshine Coast Motorway affects the majority of sites, with multiple families leaving early or losing sleep due to traffic noise beginning at 4-4:30am and continuing throughout the day, particularly impacting those in tents or light sleepers.

2. Limited facilities for older children and teenagers, with a very small pool that becomes overcrowded during busy periods, minimal activities beyond the jumping pillow and putt putt for bigger kids, and several families with teens expressing disappointment at the lack of age-appropriate entertainment options.

3. Value concerns with pricing perceived as high relative to park size and facilities, particularly the $10/night pet fee, cabin rates reaching $350/night, and higher costs than nearby competitors, with some families feeling the small pool, limited space, and highway noise didn't justify premium BIG4 pricing for this location.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"cleanest amenities by far," "marriage saver tractor is the BEST thing," "very very loud traffic noise," "pool was very small," "immaculately maintained park," "staff super friendly and helpful," "right on Maroochy River," "Wednesday pancake breakfast"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location situated between the Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach, with direct water access and walking distance to Cotton Tree village cafes and shops Outstanding natural setting with calm river waters ideal for young children and stunning sunset views, consistently praised as 'one of the best on this coastline' Generally clean and well-maintained amenities with recent improvements under new management, with many reviews noting 'spotless' and 'impeccably clean' facilities
rationale_honourable: Cotton Tree Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its exceptional beachfront location between the Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach, with families consistently praising the stunning waterfront access and proximity to Cotton Tree village shops and cafes. While the park lacks traditional entertainment facilities like pools or playgrounds, the natural beach and river environment provides ample water-based activities for children, and recent improvements to amenities and grounds maintenance show positive momentum under new management.
water_fun: Beach and river access, no pool
kids_play: No playground, natural beach play only
best_for: Families seeking a relaxed beach camping experience with young children who will enjoy the calm river swimming and patrolled beach access, where location trumps on-site facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Cotton Tree Holiday Park
SCORE: 71/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 256

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note no pool, no playground, no structured kids entertainment or activities. Families mention "no pool or other entertainment" and "not much to offer except a beach." Occasional food trucks and live music events mentioned positively. Location provides natural entertainment through beach and river access.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding natural setting praised extensively: "between the river and the beach," "right on the water," "calm waters great for kids," patrolled surf beach on one side and peaceful Maroochy River inlet on the other. Families love the "easy swim for the kids" in calm river waters. Bush turkeys mentioned frequently (sometimes as nuisance). "One of the best on this coastline" for sunsets and views.

Cleanliness (12/15) — Mixed feedback with recent improvements noted. Many praise "very clean," "spotless," "impeccably clean" amenities and "cleanest caravan park toilet I ever been." However, multiple reviews mention dirty amenities during peak times, blocked urinals, toilets running out of paper, and inconsistent cleaning schedules. Dump points reportedly overflowing during busy periods. Recent management improving standards.

Value (9/15) — Significant criticism of pricing: "$80 a night is outrageous," "very expensive," "most expensive nightly rate," "overpriced & disgusting." Families question value with no pool or entertainment at $76-84 per night. Some accept premium pricing for location: "expensive for power, water and a parking spot but very clean." Perception of poor value compared to Big4 parks with more facilities.

Site Size (5/10) — Major pain point in reviews. Overwhelming feedback: "sites are really small & close together," "crammed in like sardines," "packed in tight," "squeezed in," "tiny space," sites "too small." Many reports of difficulty reversing caravans in, cars needing to be moved, 20+ minute setup struggles. Some corner and waterfront sites noted as better. Uneven sites mentioned with tree roots and sloping ground.

Sentiment (8/10) — Polarized sentiment. Loyal long-term visitors (15-25+ years) express disappointment with new management approach. Many "will not be back" or "won't be returning" due to poor customer service, rigid policies, lack of flexibility. However, equal number praise location and plan to return: "already booked for next year," "can't wait to come back," "definitely returning." Family atmosphere and safety noted positively when not crowded.

Location (10/10) — Near-perfect scores for location. "Perfect location," "best located place," "ideal Sunny Coast spot," "prime location," "location you can't beat." Walking distance to Cotton Tree village cafes, shops, butcher, Sunshine Plaza, surf club. Direct beach and river access from sites. Close to Mooloolaba and Maroochydore CBD. Easy access without needing to drive during stays.

WHY NOT 100:
No dedicated kids entertainment or facilities (no pool, playground, jumping pillow, games room) loses significant points for family appeal - families paying premium prices expect more than just location. Site size and crowding issues with caravans "crammed in like sardines" and many reports of sites too small for modern rigs creates stressful arrival experiences. Customer service and management flexibility concerns appear frequently with reports of rude staff, rigid cancellation policies, poor communication, and lack of compassion for genuine emergencies reducing overall guest satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"right next to the river and the beach," "crammed in like sardines," "perfect location," "sites are really small and close together," "very expensive," "no pool or other entertainment," "staff are rude," "clean amenities," "walking distance to cafes and shops," "beautiful sunsets," "won't be back," "best located place to stay"

rationale_top3: Nature & Location (27 points combined): Strong natural setting and excellent Sunshine Coast location provide a quality holiday environment Cleanliness (13 points): Above-average cleanliness standards contribute to a well-maintained park experience Value & Sentiment (16 points combined): Good perceived value and positive guest sentiment indicate satisfied visitors
rationale_honourable: Pet-friendly facilities (8 points) earn recognition for accommodating families traveling with dogs, enhancing accessibility for pet owners

While the park is clean and staff are friendly, it's dated with facilities stuck in the 1980s and lacks family-specific entertainment or modern amenities, making it better suited as a convenient stopover base for Australia Zoo visits rather than a destination holiday park for families with young children.

executive_summary:
PARK: Ocean View Tourist Park
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-03-23
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 34

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (3/20) — No mention of any kids club, playground, jumping pillow, or entertainment facilities. Reviews focus on the park being quiet and peaceful with no organized activities or family entertainment options.
Nature (16/20) — Strong nature element with views of the Glass Mountains/hinterland, wildlife sightings including kookaburras and yellow-tailed black cockatoos from the deck area. Located in a peaceful natural setting with bushland surroundings, though ocean views are minimal despite the name.
Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many recent reviews praising "spotlessly clean" and "superbly clean" amenities, but offset by consistent concerns about outdated facilities, blocked drains, poor lighting in ladies toilets, and general maintenance issues from aging infrastructure.
Value (6/15) — Significant negative feedback about pricing with multiple reviews calling it "overpriced," "ludicrous," and "very expensive for basic camp ground." Rates of $50/night for powered sites and $225/night for cabins considered poor value given dated facilities.
Site Size (6/10) — Reviews mention "bit tight for parking," "small park," and limited space. One motorhome user found it "easy to park" but other reviews suggest cramped conditions with guests placed close together.
Sentiment (5/10) — Polarized reviews ranging from enthusiastic 5-stars to strong 1-star warnings. Recent reviews show declining satisfaction with facility age. Some repeat interest but many state they'd seek other accommodation.
Location (5/10) — Convenient proximity to Australia Zoo (main attraction mentioned), 15 minutes to Maleny, 25 minutes to Montville, but situated on a busy road with traffic noise. Misleading "ocean view" name as it's 20km from ocean with minimal water views.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities - no playground, pool, waterpark, kids club, or organized activities that families with young children expect from a holiday park.
2. Severely outdated facilities described as a "time capsule" from the 1980s with blocked drains, poor lighting, old amenities, and maintenance issues that detract from the guest experience despite cleanliness efforts.
3. Poor value proposition with pricing ($50-225/night) that doesn't match the basic, aging facilities, leading multiple recent reviewers to explicitly warn others away or recommend alternative parks.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"this place is a time capsule," "amenities were spotlessly clean," "very expensive for basic camp ground," "facilities clean, basic and old," "watching kookaburras, yellow tail black cockatoos," "very close to Australia Zoo," "Don't camp here," "little dated but amenities clean"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of family-friendly entertainment facilities with no camp kitchen, playground, pool, or organized kids activities mentioned across any reviews Serious concerns about new ownership practices including allegations of dishonest financial dealings, overcharging, and bond disputes that would concern family travellers Park infrastructure and operations geared toward permanent residents rather than holiday families, with very limited office hours (Monday-Friday 11am-4pm only), old basic amenities, and small sites
rationale_honourable: Located in scenic Sunshine Coast Hinterland with proximity to quaint Mapleton village offering cafes and parks, though limited nature-based activities detailed for families
water_fun: No pool or water facilities mentioned
kids_play: No kids entertainment or playground mentioned
pet_detail: No evidence in reviews regarding pet policy
best_for: Not recommended for families with young children seeking an active holiday experience with entertainment and facilities
executive_summary:
PARK: Mapleton Cabins & Caravan Park
SCORE: 38/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-12-19
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 7

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — No evidence of any on-site kids entertainment, activities, playground, or camp kitchen facilities mentioned across all reviews. Park appears focused on accommodating long-term residents rather than family holiday experiences.

Nature (14/20) — Located in scenic Sunshine Coast Hinterland with one reviewer describing "the park itself is very beautiful to stay" and close proximity to the quaint Mapleton village. However, limited detail about nature-based activities or nearby attractions for families.

Cleanliness (9/15) — Mixed feedback with some reviews noting "Amenities were very clean" while recent reviews described facilities as "very old and basic" with cleaning duties appearing to fall to long-term residents rather than staff.

Value (4/15) — Multiple reviews cited $55 per night as "Quite rich" and "pricey" for the limited facilities offered. Recent review claimed accommodation is "overpriced" with serious allegations about dodgy financial practices from new owners.

Site Size (4/10) — Sites described as "small, dark, shady and cold" by one reviewer who left early. Another mentioned being "parked in mud" initially. Limited availability for casual stays with most sites occupied by permanent residents.

Sentiment (3/10) — Heavily weighted recent reviews show declining satisfaction with new ownership. Two recent 1-star reviews cite dishonest practices and poor accessibility. One guest "left two days early" and another advised to "Go elsewhere Caravaners."

Location (8/10) — Positive mentions of being "Close to little town, cafes, parks" in the scenic Mapleton village area, though limited office hours (Monday-Friday 11am-4pm only) create accessibility challenges for weekend arrivals.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family-friendly entertainment facilities including no camp kitchen, playground, pool, or organized kids activities mentioned across all reviews.
2. Serious recent concerns about new ownership practices with allegations of dishonest financial dealings, overcharging, and bond disputes that would concern family travellers.
3. Very limited operational hours with no weekend staff presence, "very old and basic" amenities, small sites, and park infrastructure geared toward permanent residents rather than holiday families.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"NEW OWNERS ARE DODGY", "Bathroom facilities very old and basic", "No camp kitchen", "Quite rich for $55 a night", "small, dark, shady and cold", "geared towards permanent or long-term residents", "We left two days early", "Go elsewhere Caravaners"

rationale_top3: Severely hostile environment toward families with children, with reports of verbal abuse from management and permanent residents for normal play noise, causing guests to leave early and feel unsafe Critical cleanliness and maintenance failures including cockroaches in cabins, spiders, constantly blocked toilets, filthy amenities, and broken facilities described as the worst guests had experienced Complete absence of family entertainment infrastructure with no playground, kids activities, or family-friendly amenities, and management changes removing existing facilities
rationale_honourable: Strong natural rainforest setting with abundant wildlife including lizards and bush turkeys, providing good shade and authentic hinterland atmosphere for nature appreciators
water_fun: Pool mentioned but limited child-specific facilities
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment mentioned
pet_detail: Dogs mentioned in reviews but manager threatened pound for off-leash incidents
best_for: Budget-conscious single travellers or couples seeking natural rainforest setting
executive_summary:
PARK: Glen Caravan Park
SCORE: 32/100
DATE SCORED: 2025
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 17

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (3/20) — No kids entertainment facilities mentioned; one family reviewer explicitly stated park has a pool but management and permanent residents are hostile to children making normal play noise; described as "not a place for families"
Nature (16/20) — Strong natural setting with "real rainforest" environment; multiple reviews praise "tonnes of lizards and bush turkeys", palm trees, wildlife, and shaded sites; good access to Sunshine Coast Hinterland
Cleanliness (3/15) — Major cleanliness issues dominate recent reviews: cabins "full of cobwebs and spiders", "cockroaches crawling across my face", "filthy amenities", "toilets blocked constantly"; only 2 older reviews mention "spotless" amenities before ownership change
Value (6/15) — Described as "budget accommodation" with "reasonable rates" but multiple families felt it was "ridiculous" value given poor conditions; several guests left early and sought refunds
Site Size (4/10) — Mixed feedback: some mention "large sites" and "big site" but recent reviews note "very tight site", "tight parking", and "smaller side compared to other van parks"
Sentiment (2/10) — Severely negative for families; one detailed 1-star review states children were "abused" for playing, family felt "unsafe", manager was "aggressive"; multiple guests left early; permanent residents create unwelcoming atmosphere
Location (8/10) — Strong location score: "walking distance to Nambour", "close to Cole's and Aldi", "15 minutes to central Maroochydore", beaches and hinterland areas accessible

WHY NOT 100:
1. Explicitly not family-friendly with hostile environment toward children making normal play noise, resulting in families leaving early and feeling unsafe
2. Severe cleanliness and maintenance failures including cockroaches, spiders, blocked toilets, broken facilities, and run-down cabins that reviewers described as worst they'd experienced
3. Complete absence of kids entertainment, playground equipment, or family activities, with management changes removing amenities like bird cages and operational washing machines

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"not a place for families", "abused for them making noise", "cockroaches crawling across my face", "toilets blocked constantly", "worst park we have ever stayed", "filthy amenities", "Real rainforest", "wouldn't leave out a blade of grass"
